title: lewisia rediviva subsp. maguirei
intro: Lewisia rediviva subsp. maguirei, also known as Maguire's bitterroot, is a perennial plant species in the family Portulacaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its bitter properties, which are believed to aid in digestion and stimulate the appetite. The medicinal properties of Lewisia rediviva subsp. maguirei include its ability to act as a laxative, reducing inflammation, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Maguire's bitterroot can be made into teas, infusions, or capsules, and it is often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of constipation and indigestion.
